skill.name: meta-baseline-generator
skill.description: Generates a meta-analysis baseline characteristics section (text + table) from raw data. Supports Chinese and English. Use when the user provides baseline data and wants a formatted results section.
skill.summary: Generates standardized baseline characteristics sections and Markdown tables for meta-analysis papers from raw study data, supporting both Chinese and English output.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 67 static analysis findings evaluated as false positives. The 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' detections were triggered by substring matches on 'md' in 'markdown' text across all files — no cryptographic code exists in this repository. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' detections are markdown code fence formatting (```) in SKILL.md documentation and backtick characters inside regex pattern strings in text_processor.py — no shell execution occurs. The 'System reconnaissance' detections are benign text strings in documentation and audit JSON. The 'Hardcoded URL' is the source repository attribution link in SKILL.md. The skill is a straightforward text-processing tool using regex and string formatting with no network requests, code execution, file system access beyond documented usage, or data exfiltration.

content.value_statement: Manual formatting of baseline characteristics tables for meta-analyses is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill automates the generation of standardized text descriptions and Markdown tables from raw study data, supporting both Chinese and English output.
actual_capabilities: Generate descriptive text summaries of baseline study characteristics from raw data
Convert structured baseline information into formatted Markdown tables
Insert table citations at the correct position before final sentence punctuation
Support both Chinese and English output languages for international research teams
Produce reproducible, standardized results sections following academic conventions
limitations: Requires LLM-generated text and table content before the Python script can process and combine them
Only supports Chinese and English as output languages with language-specific punctuation handling
Table output is limited to Markdown format and cannot export to Word or LaTeX directly
Does not perform statistical analysis or validate the accuracy of input study data

use_cases.title: Clinical Researcher Drafting Manuscript
use_cases.description: A clinical researcher preparing a meta-analysis for journal submission needs to generate a properly formatted baseline characteristics section with consistent table formatting across multiple included studies.

use_cases.title: Medical Writer Standardizing Review Output
use_cases.description: A medical writer producing systematic reviews across multiple projects needs a repeatable method to format baseline tables from varying data sources with consistent styling.

use_cases.title: Academic Author Ensuring Reproducibility
use_cases.description: An academic author wants baseline characteristics sections generated consistently across manuscript revisions and co-author contributions without manual reformatting.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Baseline Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a baseline characteristics section for my meta-analysis titled "[Insert Title]". Here is the baseline data: [Paste JSON or text data here]. Output in English.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick generation from provided study data

prompt_templates.title: Chinese Language Output
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the baseline data below, generate the meta-analysis baseline characteristics section in Chinese. Title: [Insert Title]. Data: [Paste data]. Ensure all headings, labels, and descriptions are output in Chinese with proper punctuation.

prompt_templates.title: Full Workflow with Script Processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a baseline characteristics section from this data: [Paste data]. Title: [Insert Title]. After generating the text description and table, run the text_processor.py script to combine them with proper citation placement before the final punctuation. Language: English.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complete end-to-end generation with script combination

prompt_templates.title: Batch Multiple Analyses
prompt_templates.prompt: I have baseline data for three related meta-analyses. For each one, generate the baseline characteristics section and combine with the text_processor.py script. Analysis 1 Title: [Title 1], Data: [Data 1]. Analysis 2 Title: [Title 2], Data: [Data 2]. Analysis 3 Title: [Title 3], Data: [Data 3]. All output in English.
prompt_templates.scenario: Processing multiple meta-analyses in a single session

output_examples.input: Title: "Risk Factors for Type 2 Diabetes", Data: 12 included studies with 5,000 total patients, Baseline information in JSON format, Language: English
output_examples.output: A formatted Results section with a descriptive paragraph summarizing the 12 studies and total sample size, a Markdown table showing baseline characteristics with study names and demographic variables, and the (Table 1) citation correctly inserted before the final period.

output_examples.input: Title: "高血压治疗效果的荟萃分析", Data: 8 studies in structured text, Language: Chinese
output_examples.output: A Chinese-language Results section with study descriptions and column headers displayed in Chinese characters, a properly aligned Markdown table, and the citation tag placed before the final Chinese punctuation mark.

best_practices: Always provide complete baseline data in structured format such as JSON or organized text for the best table output quality
Specify the target language explicitly at the start of your request to ensure consistent output in all sections
Review generated outputs for accuracy before including them in manuscripts submitted for publication
anti_patterns: Do not submit unstructured or incomplete data and expect properly formatted table output with all columns filled
Do not skip the script processing step after LLM generation if you need the citation tag placed correctly before final punctuation
Do not use the skill for statistical analysis or interpretation of baseline data beyond descriptive formatting

faq.question: What types of data can this skill process?
faq.answer: The skill accepts baseline characteristics data in JSON format, structured text, or tabular format that describes study populations, sample sizes, and demographic variables across included studies.

faq.question: Does this skill perform statistical analysis on baseline data?
faq.answer: No. This skill only formats and presents baseline data as text and tables. It does not calculate statistics, perform significance tests, or interpret study findings.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for languages other than Chinese and English?
faq.answer: Currently only Chinese and English are supported. The Python script includes language-specific punctuation detection patterns for these two languages only.

faq.question: How do I provide the baseline data to the skill?
faq.answer: Include the data directly in your prompt. The skill first uses LLM generation to create the text description and table, then runs the Python script to combine and format the final output.

faq.question: What output format does the skill produce?
faq.answer: The final output is a Markdown-formatted results section containing a descriptive paragraph with citation, a table title, and a Markdown table showing baseline characteristics for all included studies.

faq.question: Is the generated content ready for journal submission?
faq.answer: The skill generates a strong first draft following academic formatting conventions. You should review the content for numerical accuracy against your source data and adjust formatting to match your target journal guidelines.
